vars.input: Approaches explored and key decisions captured in the brainstorm doc; the worktree and spec.md are written. The design is settled and we're ready to produce the file-by-file implementation plan with an ordered task list.
We chose approach B and documented the rationale and non-goals. The exploration is done; the next move is to convert these settled decisions into an ordered implementation plan with tasks.
plan.md and tasks.md are committed. Before any code is written we want to spawn parallel research agents that enrich each plan section with codebase patterns, prior art, and learnings.
The implementation plan is drafted and committed. Before writing code we want independent reviewers (DHH, Kieran, simplicity) to critique the plan's approach and flag over-engineering.
plan.md and tasks.md are reviewed and approved. There is nothing left to research or critique; it is time to start executing the first task with incremental commits.
Implementation is complete, every task in tasks.md is checked off, and the unit tests pass green. Before opening a PR we want a multi-agent pass to catch bugs and convention issues.
We just finished the GREEN implementation for a UI change under app/(dashboard). jsdom can't catch CSS-layout or nav-state regressions, so we need the auth-seeded headless browser gate before review.
The implementation is written but the test suite has six failing specs. We want to autonomously iterate diagnosis-and-fix with checkpoint commits until the whole suite is green.
All review findings are resolved and the full suite is green. The feature is verified and ready to commit, push, open the PR, and merge.
The draft PR has fourteen unresolved reviewer comments spread across several files. We want to address every thread in parallel and mark them resolved.
The review pass is otherwise clean, but it flagged that a dashboard layout change was never visually verified. Before shipping we need the auth-seeded headless browser gate on the affected route.
We're about to ship. First we need the pre-flight checks on database migrations, security headers, and lockfile integrity before merging.
The PR is approved, CI is green, and preflight passed. We're ready to merge the feature branch into main with automatic conflict resolution and worktree cleanup.
The PR just merged to main. We need to verify the deploy succeeded and that production is healthy before moving on.
Several PRs merged this week. We want to generate a formatted release summary from their labels, linked issues, and descriptions.
